description_long: This is a commercial passenger airplane with a streamlined fuselage painted primarily white and green, featuring the EVA Air livery and logo on the tail fin. The aircraft has a low-wing configuration with swept-back wings, each equipped with two underwing engines, making a total of four engines. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer with a conventional horizontal stabilizer. The landing gear is in a tricycle arrangement, with two main gear units under the wings and a nose gear at the front. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with multiple windows visible along the side for passengers. The overall design is typical of a modern wide-body jet airliner, suitable for medium to long-haul flights.
description: A white and green EVA Air commercial jet with swept low wings, four underwing engines, and a conventional tail. The airplane features a forward cockpit and tricycle landing gear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rounded nose, cockpit windows, and the full span of the swept wings with four visible engines.
description_view_1: Left-front angled view highlighting the EVA Air logo, green tail fin, and the arrangement of the engines and landing gear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section, vertical stabilizer, and the symmetry of the wings and engines.
description_view_3: Right-front angled view showing the opposite side of the fuselage, the EVA Air branding, and the landing gear deployment.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, swept-back wings with no visible winglets and a slight dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Four underwing turbofan engines, two per wing.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main gear units and a single nose gear, all extended.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, typical of commercial airliners.
